summary: Verified fix for admin login loop bug. require_admin now correctly queries admin_users by 'username' matching JWT sub. 12/12 backend pytest cases passed and full frontend E2E flow (login → dashboard → tab switch → reload) works with 0 x 401 responses.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L50 create_token param is named 'email' but now stores username in sub — rename for clarity.
No brute-force lockout on /api/admin/login (playbook item). Consider rate limiting or 5-fail lockout if hardening required.
CORS uses allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' fallback — will be rejected by browsers. Set explicit origins in production.
Login response returns raw JWT in body only (no httpOnly cookie). Frontend stores in localStorage — XSS-exposed. Acceptable for admin-only tool but note it.
@app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated in FastAPI; migrate to lifespan handlers.
server.py is ~290 lines — fine, but if it grows further, split routes and models.
seed_data_creation: None (startup seeder handles admin + default videos idempotently). Test creates a TEST_ video that is cleaned up in same test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend uses username-based admin auth. JWT sub = username. Bug (require_admin querying by 'email') is fixed and verified via /api/admin/me + /api/admin/bookings tests. Frontend AdminLogin uses data-testid admin-username / admin-password / admin-login-submit. Token key in localStorage = 'atman_admin_token'.
rca of the issue: require_admin was querying db.admin_users.find_one({'email': payload['sub']}) but the seeder now writes {'username': ...}. Every authenticated request thus hit 'User not found' → 401 → axios interceptor cleared token and redirected to /admin, appearing as a login loop. Fix: query by {'username': payload['sub']}. Verified with 12 passing backend tests and clean E2E.
